data.title: Defence Capability – VET Systems Pathways Framework
data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia is working in collaboration with the Defence sector to utilise the VET System to support the skilling requirements of the Defence sector across multiple occupations and capability domains.

stages.stageDescription:
This project aims to support Defence in addressing new and evolving skills requirements emerging from current and future Defence sector priorities as outlined in the following documents:

- 2024 National Defence Strategy
- 2024 Integrated Investment Program
- 2024 Defence Workforce Plan

Public Skills Australia will engage with key stakeholders within the Australian Defence Force and the Department of Defence to outline the education and training environment across defence, and establish how the VET System is currently being utilised. Engagement will cover a variety of areas including workforce planning, skills identification, training, credentialing and delivery of VET qualifications within the Defence sector.
This stage has not yet commenced. Details on consultation methodology and outcomes will be uploaded here when available.

#### Completion
On completion of the project, the evidence collected will inform a framework to outline the ways in which the VET System could be leveraged to support Defence in delivering national skill standards to meet new and evolving skills requirements.

data.title: Youth Volunteer Project
data.shortDescription: The Youth Volunteer project will draw on insights from survey data and facilitated workshops with youth volunteers, from across sectors such as surf lifesaving and fire and emergency services.

stages.stageDescription:
In 2025 Public Skills Australia conducted research into the workforce challenges facing the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector. 

This research identified challenges to retain youth volunteers in the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector. Findings also revealed that volunteers aged under 30 years make up the lowest proportion of volunteers in fire and emergency services. 

In response, a specific project focused on “Understanding the Youth Volunteer” was set up to better understand the specific drivers for volunteer youth attraction, recruitment and retention in Fire and Emergency Services, focused on volunteers aged under 30.  

The project will draw on insights from survey data and facilitated workshops with youth volunteers, from across sectors such as surf lifesaving and fire and emergency services. 

Key project outcomes in mid-2026 will include an insights report, to support organisations to retain and engage with their youth volunteers.

data.title: Review of Procurement and Contracting Qualifications
data.shortDescription: The review of the Procurement and Contracting qualifications will see the update of several qualifications and overall PSP Public Sector Training Package maintenance.

stages.stageDescription:
The Procurement and Contracting Stream of qualifications outline the job role requirements for professionals responsible for procuring goods and services, as well as managing both operational and strategic procurement activities across the public and private sectors. It serves as a core framework for training procurement professionals. 

These qualifications were last reviewed in 2016 and require updates to meet the emerging capability requirements of the public sector. The updated qualifications will incorporate contemporary best practices as outlined in the 2024 Commonwealth Procurement Rules and other legislative changes since 2016. 

By addressing the gaps between industry practice and existing qualifications, the project will aim to ensure training reflects current procurement methods, risk management practice, supply chain coordination and technological advancements. 

**Feedback on the draft products can be provided [here](https://forms.office.com/r/RChBZiiQqY).**

**Procurement and Contracting Qualifications**
- PSP40616 Certificate IV in Procurement and Contracting 
- PSP50616 Diploma of Procurement and Contracting 
- PSP60616 Advanced Diploma of Procurement and Contracting 
- PSP80116 Graduate Certificate in Strategic Procurement 
-  PSPSS00042 Procurement Fundamentals Skill Set 
- PSPSS00043 Delegation Skill Set 
- PSPSS00061 Procurement Basics Skill Set 
- PSP Public Sector Training Package Maintenance 

During this release of the Training Package, several additional qualifications are scheduled to be updated to replace superseded units of competency within their packaging rules. Additional supporting information for stakeholders will be provided during the consultation phase of this project to highlight changes within updated units. 

**Qualifications identified for these updates are:**
- PSP20122 Certificate II in Government 
- PSP30122 Certificate III in Government 
- PSP40122 Certificate IV in Government 
- PSP40216 Certificate IV in Court Operations 
- PSP40316 Certificate IV in Government Security 
- PSP40522 Certificate IV in Trade Measurement 
- PSP40716 Certificate IV in Heavy Vehicle Compliance 
- PSP50316 Diploma of Government Security 
- PSP50716 Diploma of Fraud Control 

**Proposed Qualifications for Deletion**

As part of the upcoming release of the PSP Public Sector Training Package, the following qualifications will be proposed for deletion:

- PSP50216 Diploma of Court Operations 
- PSP50522 Diploma of Trade Measurement  

This will be communicated and consulted on with relevant stakeholders during the development stage of the project. These qualifications have been proposed for deletion due to: 

- No enrolments / completions in the preceding three years, 
- Low usage without an identifiable specialist occupational outcome, or 
- Duplication of occupational outcomes with other native qualifications (or the broader Training Package landscape). 

### Project Phases

This project will be conducted over two phases. 

**Phase 1: Research and Industry Engagement**

This first phase of the project will see mapping of training products within the PSP Public Sector Training Package and touch points across training products that use imported units of competency. Public Skills Australia will engage industry to assess job roles, functions and future requirements in the procurement and contracting landscape to inform technical committee meetings. 

**Phase 2: Training Product Development**

For this project, Public Skills Australia has determined that two technical committees will be required, one for the Procurement and Contracting qualifications and a second committee for PSP Public Sector Training Package maintenance. Technical committee membership will meet the requirements of Training Package Organising Framework (2025). 

Technical Committees will provide be established to advice on: 

- Design and alignment of training products 
- The purpose and occupational outcomes of qualifications 
- Consideration for streamlining training products 
- Methods of consultation to maximise engagement and opportunities for stakeholder input 
- Assess implementation challenges for Registered Training Organisations 
- Advice on equivalency and transferability of units within qualification 

On commencement of Phase 2, a detailed Consultation Strategy and Technical Committee terms of reference will be made available on this page.

This first phase of the project will see mapping of training products within the PSP Public Sector Training Package and touch points across training products that use imported units of competency. Public Skills Australia will engage industry to assess job roles, functions and future requirements in the procurement and contracting landscape to inform technical committee meetings.

Phase 1 has now been completed, culminating in a comprehensive Skills Review Report. The findings from this report have informed Technical Committee discussions and guided the development of draft training products.

Consultation on the draft products will take place from May to June 2026. This process will include a mix of in-person workshops, online consultation sessions, and a structured product feedback form, providing stakeholders with multiple opportunities to contribute and provide input.

**Consultation Completed**

Consultation occurred from 5 May 2026 to 4 June 2026. 

A summary of the feedback compiled will be published here soon.

**Consultation Methodology**

An open and inclusive national consultation process that enables contributions from key users of the training products being developed is essential to the development process. This will ensure industry relevance and stakeholder support for the final products. Consultation is also intended to ensure that the Qualification Development Quality Principles are applied to training product work throughout the process.

data.title: Police Training Package Release 11
data.shortDescription:
The POL Police Training Package Release 11 will see the update to a range of qualifications to support the Policing industries workforce capability and training priorities.

Public Skills Australia will update the POL Police Training Package Release 11 in collaboration with the Australia New Zealand Policing Advisory Agency (ANZPAA), the peak body of the policing industry within Australia and New Zealand.

This project will support the supervisory and management capabilities within police agencies. Additionally, police agencies have prioritised the update of the Not for Public Access (NFPA) training products regarding Bomb Technical Response to be included in the upcoming release.

Release 11 presents a valuable opportunity to engage with a key industry partner through alignment of the qualifications with cross-jurisdictional strategies and capability development frameworks.

stages.stageDescription:
# Finalisation of Training Products 

**Release 11 of the POL Police Training Package**  

The Police Training Package Release 11 project, led by Public Skills Australia in collaboration with the Australia New Zealand Policing Advisory Agency, is in its final stage. It updates key policing qualifications to better align with current workforce needs and industry priorities, strengthens leadership and management capability, and modernises specialised areas such as bomb technical response. 

This includes updates to the following qualifications and their core units of competency:  

- POL50218 Diploma of Police Bomb Technical Response (NFPA) 

- POL58121 Diploma of Police Search and Rescue Coordination (Marine/Land) 

- POL60421 Advanced Diploma of Police Search and Rescue Management 

**Products Submitted to Assurance Body** 

A detailed list of updated products, the nature of each update, and their equivalence is available here:

- [POL 11 - Attachment A](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/dd648655-089a-4a86-8a6b-4ffc378c1993.xlsx)

- [Products](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/68a7c2b6-db3c-453f-8c2b-44441abc88cc.zip)

- [CVIG](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/7303ebad-2151-4944-8d57-ddac4c4e43dd.pdf)

- [Mapping](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/364cb71d-d253-4367-9b5d-7269923a089e.xlsx)

**Note:** Not‑For‑Public‑Access products include sensitive material and cannot be shared publicly.  

**Consultation Summary**  

The updated training products have achieved national consensus across the policing sector. Every Australian police agency has endorsed the changes, with formal approval provided by each jurisdiction’s Commissioner and the President of the Police Federation of Australia. Following public and government consultation, letters of support were also received from the Senior Responsible Officers in all states and territories. A summary of the public consultation is available here:

- [Consultation Log](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/5c438ba2-20d9-45e1-8ad1-e88d4c3b14e2.pdf)

- [Consultation Strategy](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/a9536bee-9857-497b-ae94-e921dd694644)

**Finalisation**  

As part of the training product submission process, a submission form to the Training Package Assurance Body has been prepared. The details of this can be found below: 

- [Submission Form](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/3b735880-c84d-493b-ac3b-1f0aa6a2efa4.pdf)

- [Anti-Discrimination Assessment Report](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/d2fc1a12-2edb-4ee3-bf63-faaef7184815.pdf)

The project aims to update the following qualifications:
- POL50218 Diploma of Police Bomb Technical Response (NFPA) 
- POL58121 Diploma of Police Search and Rescue Coordination (Marine/Land)
- POL60421 Advanced Diploma of Police Search and Rescue Management  

#### Project Objectives
Public Skills Australia will support the Policing Industry to update the training package by:
- Updating the Police management, leadership and Bomb Technical Response qualifications to reflect contemporary policing practice. 
- Rationalising and streamlining training products to mitigate duplication. 
- Updating training products to incorporate recent advancements in technology, as well as contemporary methods of asset detection. 
- Increasing the utilisation of qualifications within policing agencies. 


#### Technical Committee

Engagement with the Police sector is currently taking place. In accordance with the *Training Package Organising Framework (2025)*, a Technical Committee has been stood up to provide advice on amendments to the POL Police Training Package Release 11. The Technical Committee will provide expert advice, grounded in evidence to assist in the design and development of the training products.

#### Get in touch
Please check this project page periodically for further updates.
For any queries about this project, please email the Training Product Development team at tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au.
More information will be made available shortly regarding the development of Draft Training Products.

stages.stageDescription:
This project will see the development of training products to align with the workplace outcomes of Firefighting Appliance Pump Technician.

Firefighting Appliances are operational vehicles used by the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector. These appliances are designed to extinguish fires, facilitate rescues and provide various other forms of assistance. Consequently, the pumps utilised within Firefighting Appliances require specialised skills and knowledge for maintenance, troubleshooting and fault diagnosis.

In collaboration with the Australasian Fire and Emergency Services Authorities Council (AFAC), Public Skills Australia will identify the national practice standards of pump technicians and assist to build industry capacity of this workforce.

data.title: Emergency Tree Operations
data.shortDescription: This project will see the development of a Findings Report which will highlight the wide range of tree operations conducted by State Emergency Services (SES) and Fire and Emergency members, analyse their capability requirements, and consider the contextualisation of existing training products to meet these needs.

stages.stageDescription:
This project will see the development of a Findings Report which will highlight the wide range of tree operations conducted by State Emergency Services (SES) and Fire and Emergency members, analyse their capability requirements, and consider the contextualisation of existing training products to meet these needs.


Emergency Tree Operations require a wide range of skills, including an awareness of overhead hazards and the mitigation of risks within dynamic work environments. Due to the increase in severe weather events across Australia, these skills are in greater demand across the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector.


For the purposes of this report, the project will consider tree operations that include vegetation management practices in emergency contexts such as trimming, cross cutting and falling. This project will analyse existing training products that are available across different training packages for possible contextualisation within the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector.

During this phase, Public Skills Australia will collaborate with key stakeholders within the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector and other Jobs and Skills Councils to inform the development of the Emergency Tree Operations Findings Report.
This consultation phase will see Public Skills Australia engage with the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector to validate it's key findings for the report.
This phase will see the finalisation of the Emergency Tree Operations Findings Report with recommendations for future activities.

data.title: Review of Government Investigations Qualifications
data.shortDescription: The Review of the Government Investigations Qualifications will see the update of several qualifications aligned to the Australian Government Investigation Standards.

stages.stageDescription:
The Government Investigation qualification stream defines the essential competencies required to conduct official investigations and ensure regulatory compliance on behalf of government agencies. These qualifications are explicitly outlined as requirements within the Australian Government Investigation Standards 2022 (AGIS).  

The AGIS establishes a benchmark for organisations carrying out administrative, civil, or criminal investigations for state or Commonwealth government entities. As the portfolio agency of the Attorney-General’s Department, the Australian Federal Police is responsible for maintaining the AGIS to meet whole-of-government standards.  

Given the strong alignment between the AGIS and this qualification stream, it is crucial that training products are regularly reviewed and updated to remain compliant with legislative requirements and accurately reflect capability needs within the public sector.  

This project will involve a review of three qualifications and a Skill Set within this stream from the PSP Public Sector Training Package. 

**Feedback on the draft products can be provided [here](https://forms.office.com/r/RChBZiiQqY).**

**Phase 1: Research and Analysis** 

This phase focuses on the initial engagement of stakeholders to support data collection. Public Skills Australia will undertake research and analysis to assess the usability and relevance of the qualification streams. The project will seek to align the investigatory roles with the latest Investigative Standards. Stakeholder engagement will include consultation with the relevant agencies who conduct government investigations, Registered Training Organisations, and other relevant agencies. 

**Phase 2: Development** 

Throughout the development phase, Public Skills Australia will draft training products based on the advice of a Technical Committee, composed of members outlined with the Training Package Organising Framework (2025). Through Technical Committee meetings, Public Skills Australia use the data gathered from phase one to determine relevant updates. 

During this phase, further details on the Consultation Strategy and the Technical Committee’s Terms of Reference, will be made available on the website. 

**Scope of Government Investigations Qualifications**

The review and update of this stream will include the following qualifications and skill set:

PSPREG026 Review and evaluate investigations

data.title: Correctional Services Implementation Findings Report
data.shortDescription: This project will help identify the targeted support Public Skills Australia can provide Registered Training Organisations (RTOs) in delivering CSC30122 Certificate III in Correctional Practice.

stages.stageDescription:
CSC30122 Certificate III in Correctional Practice is recognised by industry as the foundational qualification to be employed as an Adult Custodial or Community Corrections Officer. This qualification is provided through a range of contexts, usually divided into phases that consist of classroom-based learning, self-paced online modules, and on-the-job training.

This project will help identify the targeted support Public Skills Australia can provide Registered Training Organisations (RTOs) in delivering this important qualification. It will also examine supports that can be offered to candidates undergoing the training and assessment as well as provide recommendations to improve and promote the delivery and implementation of the qualification.

The scope of the project includes:

- compiling a Job Role Analysis to capture candidate and industry requirements for Adult Custodial and Community Correction Officers.
- identifying the challenges and barriers faced by industry, RTOs and candidates completing CSC30122 Certificate III in Correctional Practice.
- providing recommendations that strengthen the implementation and delivery of the qualification.

To inform the project, Public Skills Australia will directly engage with RTOs and academies to collect and analyse evidence-based insights into the implementation and delivery challenges that Corrective Services academies, RTOs and candidates are currently experiencing.

Please check the project page periodically for further updates.
For any queries about this project, please email the Training Product Development team at: tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au.
Details on consultation methodology and outcomes will be uploaded here when available.
Stakeholder feedback for this project will be uploaded here once available.
Further details on the finalisation of this project will be shared here when available.

data.title: Defence Cyber Risk Assessment
data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia is working in collaboration with the Department of Defence to develop vocational training products that strengthen Australia’s cyber defence and resilience capabilities and support career pathways within the cyber domain.

stages.stageDescription:
This project responds to the growing need for cyber security expertise across Defence and Government. It focuses on improving cyber risk assessment capabilities, particularly in managing threats to Information and Communications Technology systems.

Cyber threats are evolving rapidly due to technological advancements and increased capability among state and non-state actors. These risks extend beyond typical industry concerns, encompassing national security and critical infrastructure protection.

To address these challenges, the project will develop a new credential within the DEF Defence Training Package, specifically targeting cyber security risk assessment functions. This includes international benchmarking research to understand factors of cyber resilience by leaders in the field, reviewing existing units of competency, developing new units where gaps exist, and ensuring alignment with national frameworks and interagency applicability.

By creating fit-for-purpose training solutions, this initiative will support Defence-aligned career pathways, promote cross-sectoral use of training products, and enable lateral entry for skilled workers, bolstering Australia’s resilience in the cyber domain and safeguarding national interests.


**Timeline**

Commencement: August 2025

Consultation: April – May 2026

Governance and Finalisation: June – August 2026

Completion: August 2026

*Department of Defence images are used with permission and should not be reproduced without permission from the organisation.*

Consultation will allow for meaningful involvement of stakeholders and further enhance the development process.

data.title: Local Government Skills Audit
data.shortDescription: The Local Government Skills Audit is a national initiative led by Public Skills Australia to identify vocational skills gaps across Australian Local Government Areas.

stages.stageDescription: This project aims to build an evidence base to address workforce capability challenges and recommend improvements to training pathways for Local Government. The project includes a national audit of skills data, stakeholder consultations, and analysis of the uptake and utility of existing LGA VET training packages. It will map skills shortages against current training offerings and propose strategies such as curriculum reform, RTO engagement, and policy enhancements to strengthen the skilled labour pipeline and ensure future workforce readiness in the Local Government sector.

**Consultation and data collection**
Public Skills Australia is dedicated to ensuring stakeholder needs guide project processes, outcomes and recommendations. As such, Public Skills Australia will work collaboratively with State and Federal peak bodies, RTOs and all relevant stakeholders to build a quantitative and qualitative data set to articulate the skills needs and shortages with Local Government. We are committed to adopting a flexible and agile approach to data collection and consultation that represents the unique needs of Local Governments and their stakeholders.

**Data analysis and skills mapping**
The collection of quantitative data articulating the nature and volume of skills shortages will facilitate the mapping of these shortages against the content of LGA VET products. This will allow Public Skills Australia to determine training gaps within VET products and assess opportunities for increased agility in training delivery. The qualitative data collected will inform the project of the real-world challenges faced by Local Governments including barriers in accessing training, multi-role staff models and challenges faced by rural and remote Local Governments.
The final report detailing the project findings and recommendations will be submitted to the Department of Employment and Workplace Relations in June 2026.

data.title: Volunteer Leadership Project
data.shortDescription: The Volunteer Leadership Project addresses critical capability gaps in non-operational leadership roles across Australia’s volunteer-based Fire and Emergency Services.

stages.stageDescription:
### Agency Consultation Activities

#### Consultation is closed

The Volunteer Leadership Project was established to support the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector identify the capability requirements of non-operational leaders within agencies. The findings of the project will be used to inform changes to the PUA Public Safety Training Package to support these roles.

During the initial project phase, Public Skills Australia established a Volunteer Leadership Working Group comprising members from each state and territory and across fire, State and Territory Emergency Service and Surf Life Saving agencies.

**Project Scope and Activities** 

The Volunteer Leadership Project focuses on enhancing non-operational leadership across Australia’s volunteer emergency services, including Volunteer Fire Brigades, State Emergency Services (SES), and Marine Rescue organisations. 

Key activities include: 

- Conducting a desktop environmental scan of existing leadership training models 

- Engaging stakeholders through structured consultation processes 

- Analysing data to identify common themes and capability gaps 

Insights from these activities will inform the future development or revision of training products within the PUA Public Safety Training Package, helping to build a more consistent and effective leadership framework for volunteer emergency services nationwide.
A scan of the environment through desktop research.

### Consultation sessions

A range of online meetings were held between 4 February and 15 February 2026 to gather feedback. 

### Agency Consultation Activities

We conducted face-to-face consultations for the Volunteer Leadership Project with stakeholders across Australia, which included Fire and Emergency services, Surf Life Saving, Marine Rescue and SES. 

The sessions were held across Western Australia (DFES Forrestfield), Queensland (SES Headquarters Nundah) and South Australia (SES Headquarters Keswick). The sessions focused on non-operational volunteer leadership, job role capabilities and knowledge to show our stakeholders how we are unlocking the potential of vocational education and training across Australia.

The consultations were a great opportunity for stakeholders and working groups to share their expertise and make contributions to the project. With working groups having representation around the country, covering every jurisdiction.

The findings align with two Ministerial priorities: qualifications reform and the federal government’s productivity agenda, with a focus on building a skilled, adaptable and inclusive volunteer workforce.

The Ministerial priorities have informed the development of the report, which include updates to training products embed Qualification Reform principles, undertaking activity that supports building a skilled, adaptable and inclusive workforce, and identify opportunities to strengthen capabilities of the public safety and government workforces in preparing for and responding to emergency events, including identification of emerging or revised skill requirements.

data.title: DEFEXO Defence Explosive Ordnance
data.shortDescription: This project will see the development of a new unit of competency within the DEF Defence Training Package to support the Defence sector’s capability in testing and evaluating explosive ordnance.

stages.stageDescription:
This project will see the development of a new unit of competency within the DEF Defence Training Package to support the Defence sector’s capability in testing and evaluating explosive ordnance. This project has been identified in collaboration with the Department of Defence to support the safe testing of explosive ordnance and safeguard the wellbeing of its personnel within this capability. 

Initial engagements with subject-matter experts and stakeholders in the Defence sector have identified the technical components and requirements of this specialised unit of competency.

Public Skills Australia acknowledges the use of explosive ordnance units within the mining industry-sector and will engage with the Mining and Automotive Skills Alliance (AUSMASA), to identify relevant organisations for consultation. Public Skills Australia will work closely with the Australian Defence College to support the tri-service accreditation of Australian Defence Force staff and ensure the new unit of competency is effectively implemented.

#### Technical Committee

Engagement with the Defence sector is currently taking place. In accordance with the Training Package Products Development and Endorsement Process Policy, a Technical Committee has been stood up to provide advice on amendments to the DEF Defence Explosive Ordnance qualifications. The Technical Committee will provide expert advice, grounded in evidence to assist in the design and development of the training products.

#### Terms of Reference

The Terms of Reference for the Technical Committee can be found below:

[Defence Explosive Ordnance – TOR](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/1c8c4556-af3b-401b-85dc-1a63a5f68aa1)

Note: multiple members of the Technical Committee have had their identifying information omitted from the Terms of Reference.

Consultation Strategy
Public Skills Australia will engage in thorough consultation with stakeholders to ensure that their perspectives, concerns and insights are fully considered in the development process. Details of consultation, including mechanisms and identification of stakeholders can be found here:

[Defence Explosive Ordnance – Consultation Strategy](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/916a050c-e5db-44ed-9b1f-1197fdf5aa88)

#### 13 November 2024
The consultation period for the Defence Explosive Ordnance Project has now ended. 

Based on the feedback received, Public Skills Australia will collaborate with the project’s Technical Committee to determine if any revisions to the products are required.

The project's Consultation Log, which includes feedback and responses to feedback, is now available for download on this page.


#### 15 October 2024
Public Skills Australia has developed a draft unit of competency based on advice from the Defence Explosive Ordnance Technical Committee. 

This proposed new unit of competency, **‘DEFEXOXXX Conduct explosive ordnance test and evaluation processes'** has been designed to support the Australian Defence Force’s explosive ordnance testing and evaluation capabilities. Addressing the necessary skills and knowledge, it aims to assist in providing a safer environment for personnel undertaking ordnance test and evaluation activities. 

The unit covers identifying test requirements and methods, safety considerations, developing evaluation plans and drafting reports. 

This unit applies to Defence explosive ordnance test and evaluation supervisors, managers and team leaders but is relevant to any individuals who work in this field. It has been identified to be included as an elective unit within DEF41122 Certificate IV in Explosive Ordnance Proof and Experimental.

### We want your feedback

From 15 October to 12 November 2024, Public Skills Australia will conduct virtual workshops for public consultation with stakeholders on the proposed draft training products. PSA invites you to these consultations so you can share your feedback.

### Workshop Schedule

**Session 1:** Wednesday 23 October 2024, 11:00am – 12:00pm AEDT

**Session 2:** Wednesday 30 October 2024, 11:00am – 12:00pm AEDT

data.title: Cross-Industry Biosecurity Skills Review
data.shortDescription: The Biosecurity Skills Review is a cross-JSC project to support the implementation of the National Biosecurity Strategy - an Australian Government Priority.

stages.stageDescription:
The Biosecurity Skills Review is a cross-JSC project to support the implementation of the National Biosecurity Strategy - an Australian Government Priority.

Biosecurity refers to the controls and measures to manage the risk of pests, weeds and diseases entering, emerging, establishing or spreading within Australia.  The scope of this project covers exotic and established pests, weeds and diseases, including zoonotic diseases, but does not extend to human biosecurity.

Public Skills Australia will support Skills Insight by looking at an extensive range of occupations that relate to biosecurity including standard, ancillary and surge workforces. Through research and industry consultation the report will map current biosecurity occupation and other occupations that have relevant skills and knowledge that can support biosecurity.  These will be aligned to current training offerings and any skills gaps identified.  The final report will provide recommendations for workforce development, training and the potential for a national skills capability framework to support the National Biosecurity Implementation Committee.

data.title: Auslan, Interpreting and Translating Qualification Review (Phase 1)
data.shortDescription: A review to assess the uptake, use and challenges relating to the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating qualifications.

stages.stageDescription:
## Phase 1 of this project is complete

The project team have finalised the [Auslan, Interpreting and Translating Qualification Review](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/a4b496b8-7882-413f-a5d5-7934baac2495.pdf) report. 

This recommendation report will inform Phase 2 of this project. You can view details of Phase 2 by [clicking here](https://publicskillsaustralia.org.au/projects/auslan-interpreting-and-translating-qualification-review-phase2).

The Auslan, Interpreting and Translating Qualification Review Project has been initiated in response to the Disability Royal Commission 2023 recommendation 6.2: 

*Increase the number of Auslan Interpreters and support the Deaf Census Report - Understanding Auslan.* 

Specifically, this project will explore:

- The utilisation of the Auslan training qualification, including factors that influence its adoption, accessibility, and relevance in the industry.
- The ability of Registered Training Organisations (RTOs) and Technical and Further Education (TAFE) to effectively deliver Auslan interpreter training, addressing challenges related to course content, delivery methods, and training capacity.
- The transition from training qualification to employment as an Auslan Interpreter, exploring the barriers to employment and identifying pathways to successful career entry in the interpreting field.

The project will be delivered in two phases:

**Phase 1**

This phase will be focussed on the development of a Findings and Recommendations Report to assess the uptake, use and challenges concerning the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating qualifications. It will identify key areas for improvement, and assess how the training can be better leveraged to increase overall use and effectiveness. 

Comprehensive insights and feedback, data will be collected through:
- Desktop Environmental Scan
- Facilitated workshops across Australia with key stakeholders

**Phase 2**

This phase will be aimed at the Review, Update, Promotion, and/or Implementation of the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating qualifications (hereafter referred to as Auslan qualification). It will focus on refining the relevant qualifications and promoting their broader use, delivery and implementation. 

The outcomes of this phase are expected to enhance the accessibility and quality of Auslan training, ensuring it meets the needs of both the interpreting community and the Deaf community.

data.title: Police Occupational Pathways
data.shortDescription: The Police Occupational Pathways Project was established in response to the Public Skills Australia 2025 Public Safety Workforce Insights Report.

stages.stageDescription:
The Police Occupational Pathways Project was established in response to the Public Skills Australia [2025 Public Safety Workforce Insights Report](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/8606dd9e-6351-4d5d-b595-2dcfdd27bf43). 

The report identified increased pressures on police workforce sustainability and emphasised the need to better understand the drivers of police specialist occupation shortages.   

The term “specialist occupations” refers to policing roles that focus on a specific area of law enforcement requiring advanced training beyond a general duties police officer, such as forensics, crash investigations or bomb technical response. 

To address workforce pipeline challenges associated with specialist occupations, Public Skills Australia is collaborating with Australian policing agencies and the Australian New Zealand Policing Advisory Agency to: 
- Identify drivers that may be limiting specialist occupation capacity across policing agencies. 
- Design innovative pathways that recognise employees existing skills to increase employee transferability into specialist occupations. 
- Provide policing agencies with insights into the common skills across policing to support pathways between specialist occupations.  
- Determine how the Vocation Education and Training (VET) system could support efforts to address shortages in specialist occupations. 
- Provide recommendations (if relevant) to streamline training package products to increase inter-industry skills transferability.

Public Skills Australia held online consultations in March 2026 with all Australian policing agencies. These consultations examined how policing agencies manage training for their specialist occupations and identified associated workforce challenges.  

In-person consultations will be conducted with all policing agencies and unions in May 2026 to support the development of enhanced training pathways into and between specialist occupations.

The Project will analyse the POL Police Training Package for the 21 VET qualifications that align with specialist occupations to: 
- Identify common skills 
- Determine the extent to which those skills overlap across qualifications 
- Map patterns of capability transfer

On completion of the Project, a Pathways Documentation product will be developed to support policing agencies: 

**Understand which specialist occupations have the greatest overlap of skills**.  This will assist in guiding career pathways for personnel seeking to transition between specialist occupations and enable stronger transfer of capability. 

**Identify elements and performance criteria across the 21 VET qualifications that have a high degree of similarity**. This will allow training for one qualification to be designed and delivered in a way that also satisfies the requirements of other related qualifications. 

**Recommend new training products to upskill personnel across multiple specialist occupations**. This will support policing agencies to increase workforce mobility.

data.title: VET Workforce Study – Public Safety and Government industry-sectors
data.shortDescription: This project examines the evolving workforce capability needs across the Public Safety and Government sectors and identifies practical, evidence-based actions to strengthen and sustain the VET workforce.

stages.stageDescription:
The Public Safety and Government industry-sectors are operating in an environment of rapid and complex change. 

Increasing natural disasters, climate impacts, geopolitical uncertainty, technological transformation, and shifting community expectations are driving more complex capability requirements, placing growing pressure on the Vocational Education and Training (VET) Workforce to deliver timely, relevant and high-quality training. 

The VET system is a key mechanism for equipping current and future workforces with current and emerging skills.  

In 2024, the Department of Employment and Workplace Relations (DEWR), acting in support of the Skills and Workforce Ministerial Council, released the VET Workforce Blueprint, signalling the national priority to transform, strengthen and streamline the VET Workforce model. 

In response, DEWR commissioned all Jobs and Skills Councils (JSCs) to undertake a VET Workforce Study in their respective industries; an initiative designed to identify practical, evidence-informed actions to grow, support and sustain the VET Workforce across Australia. 

Public Skills Australia, as the JSC supporting the Public Safety and Government industry-sectors, has delivered the Study through three interconnected work streams. 

Each stream has built progressively toward a deeper understanding of the VET Workforce and the actions required to better support, strengthen and sustain it.

Public Skills Australia’s VET Workforce Study is being delivered through three interconnected work streams.  

Streams 1 and 2 (concluded Dec 2025) provided the foundational evidence base. Extensive in-person consultations have occurred with industry-sector stakeholders in every State and Territory in Australia as well as with Public Skills Australia’s strategic network groups across 2025 – 2026. Further information on all project streams is provided below.  

**Stream 1** developed a comprehensive understanding of the learning and organisational development ecosystems across the Public Safety and Government industry-sectors, identified key challenges impacting maintaining the VET Workforce and outlined opportunity areas to strengthen the responsiveness and sustainability of the VET Workforce 

**Stream 2** focused on validating Stream 1 findings and conducting explorative, in-field / industry-sector research to identify shared approaches that can inform development and design of collaborative industry-led responses – leveraging from the opportunities identified in the Stream 1 Report.  

**Stream 3** (Jan – June 2026) focuses on integrating insights and findings from Streams 1 and 2 into a practical, ADDIE-based guide, supported by case studies that illustrate good practice across the learning and development lifecycle.

Research has identified the following opportunity areas to strengthen the responsiveness and sustainability of the VET workforce in the Public Safety and Government industry-sectors: 

- Integrating operational expertise with training capability, bringing together operational staff / personnel, and training experts to build a balanced training workforce model that supports consistent, high-quality delivery. 

- Leveraging the Certificate IV in Training and Assessment (Certificate IV TAE), and broader adult-education qualifications to strengthen facilitation capability and deliver more impactful learning. 

- Enhancing proactive and continuous training delivery, supported by stronger strategic planning, systems, tools and data to anticipate future capability needs. 

- Strengthening attraction and retention of the VET Workforce through clearer and more attractive career pathways and understanding of workforce drivers and barriers. 

- Expanding coaching and mentoring arrangements to support trainer capability, effective performance, and encourage professional growth, including alignment with the Certificate IV TAE to explore opportunities for mentoring, coaching and on‑the‑job learning to contribute to qualification attainment.

Stream 3 consolidates the research, validation and co‑design completed throughout Streams 1 and 2 and will translate the findings into this Stream 3 Final Report – Learning and organisational development (the Report). This Report will serve as a practical guide detailing good practice in the implementation of the ADDIE model (or other relevant learning and development model) – Analysis, Design, Development, Implementation and Evaluation.  

![](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/44160c7f-e54e-47b0-9f96-2cbdfb9b2a5c)

*Figure: ADDIE model*

It will articulate good practice through each of the phases of the ADDIE model to support industry-sectors in enhancing learning and organisational development approaches. Each chapter will be underpinned with case studies drawn from consultations, illustrating the application of good practice and demonstrating how the principles identified can be applied across diverse operational contexts.

The report will further detail recommendations and pilot initiatives that could support the growth and sustainability of the VET Workforce within the Public Safety and Government industry.

The final report is due to the Department of Employment and Workplace Relations 31 July 2026 and is expected to be approved for publishing on this website late 2026.

data.title: Complex Infrastructure
data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia is working in collaboration with Fire and Emergency Services across Australia to address the emerging and complicating risks faced by responding members.

stages.stageDescription:
This project was initiated through consultation with Fire and Emergency Services which highlighted complex factors that pose increased risks to emergency responders. 
These factors include:
- Increased use of combustible and synthetic materials in construction
- Electric vehicle charging stations and infrastructure, especially in sub-basements of multi-level buildings
- Confined or limited access environments, including high rise buildings, tunnels, and large volume spaces that complicate fire behaviour patterns and management of fire, smoke and gases.

The project sets out to define current and emerging skills and knowledge requirements when dealing with emergencies by facilitating conversations within and between agencies on best practices to support safer and more effective preparedness and response capability.

On completion of the project, the evidence collected will inform the next steps which may result in updates, if necessary, to the PUA Public Safety Training Package.
Public Skills Australia is engaging with Industry regarding this project. Details on engagement and outcomes will be uploaded here when available.
This stage has not yet commenced. Details on consultation methodology and outcomes will be uploaded here when available.
On completion of the project, the evidence collected will inform the next steps which may result in updates, if necessary, to the PUA Public Safety Training Package.

data.title: Correctional Services Training Implementation (Phase 2)
data.shortDescription: This project strengthens delivery of CSC30122 Certificate III in Correctional Practice and CSC30222 Certificate III in Immigration Detention Operations by improving clarity, assessment quality and cultural appropriateness.

stages.stageDescription:
### Background

Phase 1: Consultation with stakeholders identified misalignment between training and practice, limited assessment opportunities, learner support challenges, and uncertainty in culturally sensitive units.

Phase 2 will see the implementation of the recommended strategies of Phase 1.

### Project Objectives

- Improve alignment with operational practice 
- Strengthening assessment approaches 
- Enhance learner support 
- Improve cultural awareness 
- Facilitate national collaboration through Good Practice Forums 


### Key Deliverables 

- Training and assessment guidance
- Implementation Guide:

Broad consultation and promotion will be conducted with industry and RTO stakeholders, established networks and the Correctional Services Subcommittee. 

Feedback from consultations will be incorporated into the Implementation Guide. 

This will include building consensus between key stakeholder groups and determining the finalised content.

In response to recommendations 1 and 3 within the Correctional Services Training Implementation Report, Public Skills Australia will develop a new Implementation Guide.

This guide will be tailored for the CSC30122 Certificate III in Correctional Practice and CSC30222 Certificate III in Immigration Detention Operations qualifications, incorporating culturally appropriate learning and assessment guidance, including:

- Interpretation for the core units of both qualifications 
- Interpretation of selected CSC Correctional Service Training Package elective units of the qualifications 
- Contextualisation support for designing resources and assessment tools 
- Strategies for flexible and valid assessment design in operational settings 
- Learning support approaches tailored to correctional and detention operational settings 
- Principles for culturally safe and trauma-informed training and assessment in correctional contexts 
- Practical examples of contextualisation and assessment strategies of these units 
- Trainer resources for guidance on cultural considerations during delivery and assessment

In response to recommendation 2 within the [Correctional Services Training Implementation Report](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/ccc807a0-48e7-41b5-8edc-95344f6f54d5.pd), Public Skills Australia will facilitate a series of events to foster national collaboration, build capacity and address implementation challenges previously identified.  

The Good Practice Forums will:  
- Focus on a specific theme relevant to the delivery and assessment of the qualifications in scope of the project  
- Promote peer learning, sharing of good practice and contextualised delivery approaches  
- Support consultation efforts for the Implementation Guide, where appropriate 

The Good Practice Forums will take place periodically throughout 2026 until January 2027.
Publishing the Implementation Guide will take place during this phase.

At this stage, internal governance procedures are conducted, and developed materials will be submitted to Department of Employment and Workplace Relations for consideration.

Details will be shared here when available.

data.title: Police Crash Investigation and Analysis Review
data.shortDescription: POL Police Training Package Release 10.0 will see the update of two qualifications within the Crash Investigation qualification stream.

stages.stageDescription:
### Project Overview

POL Police Training Package Release 10.0 will see the update of two qualifications within the Crash Investigation qualification stream. These updates will ensure that the products reflect the current and emerging requirements of police agencies. The Crash Investigation stream of qualifications outlines the skills of police officers to investigate and interpret road incidents involving a variety of vehicle types, pedestrians and other external environmental factors. This includes officers who manage incidents, gather evidence and provide their analysis for the investigation.

The update to the POL Police Training Package will be conducted in collaboration with the Australia New Zealand Policing Advisory Agency (ANZPAA), the peak body of the policing industry within Australia and New Zealand.

Through the update of the Crash Investigation qualification stream, Public Skills Australia will support the Policing industry by:

- Increasing the use of products and ensuring they are implementable.
- Improving police officers’ safety through alignment with current practices and processes.
- Enhancing the capability of Police personnel to use evaluation and analysis techniques for motor vehicle incidents.

#### Technical Committee

Engagement with the Police sector is currently taking place. In accordance with the Training Package Products Development and Endorsement Process Policy, a Technical Committee has been stood up to provide advice on amendments to the POL Police Crash Investigation and Analysis qualifications. The Technical Committee will provide expert advice, grounded in evidence to assist in the design and development of the training products.

#### Terms of Reference

The Terms of Reference for the Technical Committee can be found below:

[Technical Committee Terms of Reference](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/7778c379-9a2d-4ed2-b242-22f6a73c8a58)

Note: multiple members of the Technical Committee have had their identifying information omitted from the Terms of Reference.

#### Consultation Strategy 

Public Skills Australia will engage in thorough consultation with stakeholders to ensure that their perspectives, concerns and insights are fully considered in the development process. Details of consultation, including mechanisms and identification of stakeholders can be found here:

[Consultation Strategy](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/423c658c-96cd-4037-a4ee-06d40bf9080f)

#### Training Products in Scope

The review of the POL Police Training Package Release 10.0 include the following training products:

- **POL50120 Diploma of Crash Investigation**
- POLINV020 Collate and review crash investigation evidence
- POLINV022 Develop and present crash findings
- POLINV023 Examine a crash incident
- **POL60120 Advanced Diploma of Crash Analysis**
- POLINV016 Analyse complex factors involved in crash incidents
- POLINV017 Analyse crashes involving heavy vehicles
- POLINV018 Analyse crashes involving motorcycles
- POLINV019 Analyse crashes involving pedestrians and bicycles
- POLINV021 Critically analyse complex crash incident evidence
- POLINV024 Manage quality of crash analysis
- POLINV025 Present evidence on complex crash incidents

During the review, the packaging rules of the qualifications will be updated to ensure the units within the qualifications are all current and deliverable by Registered training Organisations.

#### Timeline

![](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/2eaacf85-397b-4ae5-95a0-63063febe709)

For any queries about this project, please email the Training Product Development team at: tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au.

#### 08/04/2025:
The consultation period for the Police Crash Investigation and Analysis project has now ended.
Public Skills Australia is currently incorporating the latest feedback received during this period.
The Consultation Log, which includes feedback and responses, will soon be available for download on this page.

Thank you to everyone who participated in this valuable process.
 
For any questions regarding the project, please contact our Training Product Development team at: tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au

The updates to the qualifications and units of competency within the POL Police Training Package Release 10.0 have progressed through ANZPAA’s governance process, and have been reviewed by:

data.title: Defence Youth Development
data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia is working in partnership with the Australian Defence College to develop vocational training products that create career and learning pathways into the Australian Defence Force (ADF), Defence industry, and the Australian Public Service (APS).

stages.stageDescription:
The Defence Youth Development Project has been successfully initiated and delivered in partnership with key stakeholders, including the Australian Defence College. The original objective to design and update vocational education and training (VET) products that support youth pathways into Defence, Defence industry and the Australian Public Service has been achieved.

This project has developed two entry-level qualifications suitable for youth cohorts in the DEF Defence Training Package. These have been published on the National Training Register.

- *DEF10126 Certificate I in Defence Skills*
- *DEF20326 Certificate II in Defence Skills*

The newly created DEFSS00049 Foundational Military Skill Set contains units that have military training significance that are not suitable for youth programs.

These qualifications provide foundational employability and life skills for young Australians, particularly those participating in VET in Schools, Cadets, and other youth development programs. They support pathways into Defence, the Defence industry, and broader public sector roles.

All planned governance, scoping and stakeholder engagement activities were completed, establishing a strong foundation for the development of relevant and contemporary training products.

**Project Overview**

This project has been established to support young Australians engaged in Vocational Education and Training (VET) in Schools, as well as those participating in the Australian Defence Force Cadets and other Defence-affiliated youth development programs. Collectively, these programs engage over 30,000 participants nationwide. They provide structured, military-style experiences underpinned by Defence values and delivered in collaboration with local communities, providing a strong foundation for future employment and national service.

Existing regulatory and packaging rule constraints currently prevent the DEF21115 Certificate II in Military Skills from being delivered through VET in Schools and other youth development programs, highlighting the need to modernise the qualifications to ensure contemporary alignment with safety standards while supporting Defence-aligned pathways. 

This project aims to redesign the packaging rules of the qualifications to connect youth development with real-world career opportunities, strengthening Australia’s future workforce across Defence and public service sectors.

This project aims to redesign the packaging rules of the qualifications, broadening pathways into Defence thorough unique experiences and engaging training opportunities for young people, strengthening Australia’s future workforce across Defence and public service sectors.

**Scope**

This project will see the update of two entry level qualifications within the DEF Defence Training Package, including:

- *DEF10117 Certificate I in Defence Skills*
- *DEF21115 Certificate II in Military Skills*

**Timeline**

Commencement: August 2025

Consultation: November – December 2026

Governance and Finalisation: January – March 2026 

Completion: April 2026


**Technical Committee Nomination**

Technical Committee Nominations have now closed.

### Consultation Completed

Consultation occurred from 12 November 2025 to 15 January 2026. A summary of the feedback compiled is found in the Consultation Log [here](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/1dec1297-e9dc-4f6e-aedb-44a985a44b5c).

#### Objective of Consultation
Public Skills Australia has worked with the Department of Defence and the Australian Defence College to establish collaborative partnerships aimed at enhancing the development of training products. By engaging with the Department of Defence and the Australian Defence College, Public Skills Australia leveraged their specialised knowledge and experience in Defence education and training. Consultation allowed for meaningful involvement of stakeholders and further enhanced the development process.

#### Consultation Plan

| Commencement of Project | Publication of project overview information | Upload the Annual Training Product Development Plan to the Public Skills Australia website. |
|    |   | Upload project overview information on the Public Skills Australia website. |

Due to the sensitivity of information sharing within Police, Technical Committee member names will not be published, only organisation, jurisdiction and job title. | Email notification to STAs and SROs and to relevant JSCs. |
| Public and Government Consultation | Provide stakeholders with workshop registration forms that detail the dates and times of virtual sessions, as well as the qualifications and units of competency they wish to review. Once consultation begins, these forms will be directly accessible from each project’s Consultation page on Public Skills Australia’s website. | Public Skills Australia to provide access to draft training products and a survey for feedback.  |
|    | Conduct public information sessions webinars.  | Provide a link to register interest for information sessions on Public Skills Australia website |

#### Stakeholder Identification
Key stakeholders have been drawn from:

Endorsement was received in May 2026.

data.title: Emerging Technology Skills Review
data.shortDescription: A review identifying the skills and capabilities Australia’s Fire and Emergency Services need to safely and effectively respond to emerging emergencies involving electric vehicles and high‑powered lithium‑ion batteries.

stages.stageDescription:
The 2025 Public Safety Workforce Insights Report by Public Skills Australia identified operational skills gaps within fire and emergency services in responding effectively and efficiently to emergencies involving emerging technologies. The increasing frequency and complexity of emergencies involving high-powered lithium-ion batteries require skills and knowledge to increase the capability of personnel and to maintain their safety. 

This project will focus on the emerging skill requirements for responding to emergencies and emergency events involving high-powered lithium-ion batteries and Electric Vehicles (EVs). EVs include road vehicles and light electric vehicles such as electric bikes, scooters and other relevant transport devices. 

This project will build upon work led by the Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW) in response to the National Electric Vehicle Strategy (2023).

In collaboration with AFAC, an analysis of existing research and doctrine will be conducted. The aim of this stage is to summarise and analyse existing research efforts within this area. To inform this work, engagements with the AFAC Alternative and Renewable Energy Technologies (ARET) Technical Group will be conducted.

This stage will see the development of a Working Group to guide the Skills Review. This group will help determine the breadth, depth and relevance of skills required within Fire and Emergency Services.

During this stage, the working group’s findings will be presented to relevant stakeholders for validation. Given the high level of public interest, from both industry and the wider community, this project will prioritise in-person engagement with stakeholders. To ensure inclusive participation from stakeholders in regional and remote locations, virtual sessions will also be offered. 

The validation will be conducted through a national roadshow targeting: 
- Volunteer and Career Fire Agencies 
- Emergency Service Organisations 
- United Firefighter Union Representatives 
- Relevant Jobs and Skills Councils 
- Relevant State and Territory Government Stakeholders  
- Registered Training Organisations  
- Interested higher education institutions.  

These sessions are designed to collect and validate both skills and knowledge requirements of Fire and Emergency Service agencies concerning new and emerging technologies.

During this stage, the Skills Review report will be finalised, summarising the project’s key findings and recommendations. The report will highlight opportunities to update specific areas of the PUA Public Safety Training Package.

data.title: POL Police Training Package Release 9.0

stages.stageDescription:
POL Police Training Package Release 9.0 will see the update to several qualifications to meet the current and emerging requirements of the Police industry-sector. The update to the POL Police Training Package will be conducted in collaboration with the Australia New Zealand Policing Advisory Agency (ANZPAA). ANZPAA is the peak body for the Policing industry-sector within Australia and New Zealand and is representative of each policing jurisdiction. 

The qualifications identified for update in this project have been informed by the ANZPAA Annual Business Plan, approved by the Australia New Zealand Council of Police Professionalisation (ANZCoPP). The ANZCoPP Board comprises of commissioners from each state and territory and is inclusive of the Police Federation of Australia.  

This project will see the review of two vital operational streams within policing, investigation and intelligence.

#### Technical Committee

Engagement with the Police industry-sector is currently taking place. In accordance with the Training Package Products Development and Endorsement Process Policy, Technical Committees has been established to provide advice on the endorsement of training products.

data.title: Auslan, Interpreting and Translating Qualification Review (Phase 2)
data.shortDescription: This project has now moved into Phase 2. It is focused on putting the validated recommendations into practice by updating the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating qualifications in accordance with the Training Package Organising Framework.

stages.stageDescription:
**Nominations for this Technical Committee are now closed.**

We have received nominations for the technical committee for this project. This committee will be established to provide advice on the design of qualifications. 

Details of this committee will be published on our website shortly. 

If you are not selected for the Technical Committee you can still contribute to these updates. To help **provide feedback or highlight challenges** related to the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating Qualification Review Project, [click here](https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/zUjQEa14HU). 

To **stay informed regarding the progress of this project**, [click here to register your interest](https://forms.office.com/r/SeTHSgVmwJ).

<iframe src="https://player.vimeo.com/video/1174961860?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479" width="1024" height="768" frameborder="0" align="left" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen"></script></iframe>

*A summary of the video is provided below.*

Public Skills Australia is conducting a review of the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating qualifications to support the recommendations within the Disability Royal Commission. Because of the importance of this project, it has been broken into two phases. 

**Phase 1**

This first phase involved stakeholder engagement to gather insights and determine the scope of changes required. The outcomes of this engagement have been compiled within the [Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating Qualifications Review report](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/a4b496b8-7882-413f-a5d5-7934baac2495.pdf). 

Stakeholder insights were categorised into three parts: 

- Employment and Community insights 
- Training Delivery Considerations 
- Training Package Considerations  

The findings and recommendations within the report were consulted and validated by stakeholders in late 2025. The recommendations within the report align to the remit of Public Skills Australia as a Jobs and Skills Council. This is predominantly to update and maintain Training Packages. 

**Phase 2**

The second phase of this project will implement the findings and recommendations from Phase One using advice from a Technical Committee, established to inform the review of the Auslan qualifications. 

It will focus on refining the relevant qualifications and promoting their broader use, delivery and implementation. 

The outcomes of this phase are expected to enhance the accessibility and quality of Auslan training, ensuring it meets the needs of both the interpreting community and the Deaf community.

Drafts of the reviewed Auslan qualifications will be made available publicly for feedback before being finalised.

As part of the federal government’s requirements to update training packages, a technical committee must be established to provide advice on the design of qualifications. 

This project's committee will be established by seeking subject matter experts, educational experts, RTO representatives, union representatives, and government representatives with relevant experience.  

Sessions will be held in an accessible format, and Interpreters will be arranged by Public Skills Australia. 

Due to departmental policy, Technical Committee membership is voluntary, and no renumeration can be offered for participation. 

**Please note that nominations for this Technical Committee have now closed, and session planning has commenced.**

Public Skills Australia will develop draft training products informed by advice from the Technical Committee, as established under the Training Package Organising Framework (2025). 

As part of this process, Public Skills Australia will present the findings and recommendations from Phase 1, specifically the Auslan, Interpreting and Translating Qualification Review report. These recommendations will support the Technical Committee in identifying the required updates to qualifications, skill sets and units of competency. 

The outcomes of these discussions will guide the drafting of revised training products to ensure they align with industry expectations and contemporary job roles.
Details on consultation methodology and outcomes will be provided here when available.
The stakeholder review will be conducted after consultations have taken place.
Further details on the finalisation of this project will be shared here when available.
